Certified Innovation Professional Level 0: Innovation Champion
Professionals take their first structured step into innovation, learning its core tools and mindset

The Certified Innovation Professional - Innovation Champion certification is designed for professionals beginning their innovation journey. Participants get acquainted with business innovation and learn the key tools and techniques of innovation management to take their first step toward becoming innovation practitioners.
© 2008-2026 Global Innovation Management Institute. All rights reserved.

A live cohort fast-tracks working professionals to CIP certification,
the only innovation management course accredited as 17024
The Certified Innovation Professional (CIP) program by
helps professionals build practical innovation knowledge, tools, and methodologies to drive innovation within organizations. It includes two levels:
Level 1 Associate: Covers innovation fundamentals, tools, and skills to develop effective innovation ideas and results.
Level 2 Master: Focuses on applying these skills to solve real-world challenges and create innovation opportunities for organizations.

High-school students learn to think more innovatively and solve problems with empathy

The Certified GIMI Impact: Students certification is designed for high school students who want to be more innovative and compassionate. Participants learn how to apply creativity and structured problem-solving to address complex challenges around the world.

Experienced innovators become certified to train and develop others

The Certified GIMI Trainer certification is designed for certified GIMI individuals who want to train others on GIMI methodologies, whether inside or outside their organization. Participants develop mastery in delivering GIMI frameworks and tools, learn to explain key innovation concepts clearly, and use GIMI's materials to build a training and certification business in innovation management.

Anyone can shift from passively watching change to proactively driving value

The Innovation Primer certification is designed for individuals who want to transform from passive observers of change into proactive drivers of value. Participants learn that innovation is not a mysterious spark of genius but a disciplined capability, building the skills to connect disparate "dots" — knowledge, research, and diverse perspectives — to create a brighter future for their clients, organization, and career.
© 2008-2026 Global Innovation Management Institute. All rights reserved.
Practitioners master a repeatable five-step method to turn problems into breakthrough ideas

The Problem Solving Certified Catalyst is designed for individuals from all backgrounds who want to develop a structured approach to creative problem-solving. Participants learn innovation tools, techniques, and an innovative mindset through a purpose-driven, five-step ideation process — Setup, Diverge, Connect, Converge, and Emerge — moving from challenge selection to actionable solution concepts and pitch-ready brochures.
© 2008-2026 Global Innovation Management Institute. All rights reserved.

Professionals build human-centred design skills through the Immerse, Invent, Implement method

The Certified Design Thinking Level 1 certification is designed for individuals who want to master human-centered design to solve complex problems and improve user experiences. Participants build a strong foundation in Design Thinking through its three stages: Immerse (capturing user insights and building empathy), Invent (defining problem statements and generating creative solutions), and Implement (prototyping, testing, and iterating).
© 2008-2026 Global Innovation Management Institute. All rights reserved.
* Cert + Training price applies to online training for cohorts of min. 15-20 people; for onsite delivery, pricing is on a case-by-case basis.
Practitioners apply Design Thinking methods to a real organisational challenge

The Certified Design Thinking Level 2 certification is designed for individuals ready to apply Design Thinking methods to real organizational challenges. Participants document their process, insights, and outcomes in a white paper that showcases how they used the Immerse, Invent, and Implement stages, gaining the skills and confidence to lead Design Thinking across diverse contexts.

Leaders turn innovation into a repeatable competency through the SCD framework
